---
title: "03 — Functions, Loops, Apply, Debugging"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

## Custom functions

```{r}
square <- function(x) x^2
square(5)
```

```{r}
# Default arguments
power <- function(x, n = 2) x^n
power(3) # 9
power(3, 3) # 27
```

```{r}
# Multiple return values via list
stats <- function(x) {
  list(mean = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE), n = sum(!is.na(x)))
}
stats(c(1, 2, NA, 4, 5))
```

## apply family

```{r}
m <- matrix(1:12, nrow = 3)
apply(m, 1, sum) # MARGIN=1: per row
apply(m, 2, sum) # MARGIN=2: per column
```

```{r}
lapply(1:3, function(x) x^2) # always returns list
sapply(1:5, function(x) x^2) # simplifies to vector when possible
vapply(1:5, function(x) x^2, numeric(1)) # safe sapply with declared return type
tapply(mtcars$mpg, mtcars$cyl, mean) # apply by group (like group_by + summarise)
```

## Iterating over files (specdata pattern)

```{r eval=FALSE}
files <- list.files("data/specdata", full.names = TRUE)

# Load and combine all files into one data frame
dat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(files[1:10], read.csv))

# pollutantmean — canonical pattern
pollutantmean <- function(directory, pollutant, id = 1:332) {
  files <- list.files(directory, full.names = TRUE)
  dat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(files[id], read.csv))
  mean(dat[[pollutant]], na.rm = TRUE)
}

# complete — using sapply over file indices
complete <- function(directory, id = 1:332) {
  files <- list.files(directory, full.names = TRUE)
  nobs <- sapply(id, function(i) sum(complete.cases(read.csv(files[i]))))
  data.frame(id = id, nobs = nobs)
}

# corr — for loop with threshold
corr <- function(directory, threshold = 0) {
  files <- list.files(directory, full.names = TRUE)
  out <- numeric(0)
  for (f in files) {
    d <- read.csv(f)
    dc <- d[complete.cases(d), ]
    if (nrow(dc) > threshold) out <- c(out, cor(dc$sulfate, dc$nitrate))
  }
  out
}
```

## for loop

```{r}
results <- numeric(5)
for (i in 1:5) results[i] <- i^2
results
```

## complete.cases

```{r}
df <- data.frame(x = c(1, NA, 3), y = c(4, 5, NA))
df[complete.cases(df), ]
sum(complete.cases(df))
```

## Benchmarking

```{r}
system.time({
  x <- rnorm(1e6)
  mean(x)
})
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(microbenchmark)
microbenchmark(
  loop = {
    s <- 0
    for (i in 1:1000) s <- s + i
  },
  sum_r = sum(1:1000),
  times = 100
)
```

## Debugging

```{r eval=FALSE}
# traceback() — call stack after an error
f <- function(x) log(x)
g <- function(x) f(x - 10)
g(5)
traceback()
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# browser() — pause execution, enter interactive mode
my_fun <- function(x) {
  browser() # stops here; n=next, c=continue, Q=quit, s=step into
  x * 2
}
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# debug() / undebug() — like browser() without modifying the function
debug(lm)
lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)
undebug(lm)
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# tryCatch — handle errors and warnings
result <- tryCatch(
  {
    log(-1)
  },
  warning = function(w) {
    message("Warning: ", conditionMessage(w))
    NA
  },
  error = function(e) {
    message("Error: ", conditionMessage(e))
    NA
  }
)
```

## testthat

```{r message=FALSE}
library(testthat)
expect_equal(2 + 2, 4)
expect_equal(sqrt(2)^2, 2, tolerance = 1e-10)
expect_true(is.numeric(3.14))
expect_error(log("abc"))
expect_warning(log(-1))
```

```{r message=FALSE}
test_that("mean ignores NA", {
  expect_equal(mean(c(1, 2, NA), na.rm = TRUE), 1.5)
  expect_true(is.na(mean(c(1, NA))))
})
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
library(profvis)
profvis({
  x <- rnorm(1e5)
  for (i in 1:100) mean(x)
})
```
